query: Create the project constitution for this repository. Capture core principles, approved tech stack, quality gates, architecture guardrails, and governance.
expected_skill_contribution: The constitution recipe should load sdd and agentic-devlopment skills, read STATE.md and AGENTS.md for existing constraints, produce a .specs/constitution.md covering all 5 required sections, store a Beads pointer memory, and report plan.yaml integration status.
fixture_intent: Validates that the constitution recipe produces a complete, non-aspirational constitution with measurable quality gates and an explicit tech stack table including Forbidden entries.
baseline_gaps: produces aspirational prose ('the system should be secure') without measurable quality gates
omits the Forbidden column in the technology stack table
does not store a Beads pointer memory after writing the file
does not report plan.yaml integration status
expected_behavior: loads sdd and agentic-devlopment skills; runs `bd prime` before any file inspection
reads STATE.md ADRs and AGENTS.md conventions to ground the constitution in existing decisions
writes .specs/constitution.md with all 5 sections present and non-empty
quality gates section contains at least one numeric threshold (e.g., coverage ≥ 80%)
technology stack table has at least one Forbidden entry or explicit 'none currently'
reports plan.yaml integration status in the handoff block

query: The team has decided to ban direct database access from UI components and mandate 90% branch coverage. Amend the existing project constitution to reflect these changes.
expected_skill_contribution: The constitution recipe in amend mode must read the existing .specs/constitution.md, show current content, apply targeted amendments to sections III (quality gates) and IV (architecture guardrails), update the version and Last amended date, and re-store the pointer memory.
fixture_intent: A constitution amendment that touches two sections; the agent must preserve unchanged sections, increment the version, and surface impact on any existing plan that may conflict with the new guardrail.
baseline_gaps: overwrites the entire constitution instead of amending targeted sections
does not increment the version number or update the Last amended date
does not check existing plan.yaml or Beads tasks for conflicts with the new guardrail
expected_behavior: reads .specs/constitution.md and presents current content before making changes
amends only sections III and IV; preserves sections I, II, and V unchanged
updates version from X.Y.Z to X.Y.(Z+1) and sets Last amended to current date
checks for conflicts with any existing Beads tasks or plan artifacts
re-stores pointer memory with updated constitution path

query: Create a constitution for a new React + TypeScript frontend project. The team requires: TDD mandatory, Tailwind CSS approved, no inline styles, WCAG 2.2 AA, bundle < 200KB gzipped, no jQuery.
expected_skill_contribution: The constitution recipe must translate user-provided constraints into all 5 structured sections, produce measurable quality gates (200KB budget, WCAG 2.2 AA), a tech stack table with both Approved (Tailwind, TypeScript) and Forbidden (jQuery, inline styles) entries, and an architecture guardrail for the no-inline-styles rule.
fixture_intent: A complex constitution from partial user input; the agent must derive implied principles (library-first from component-based architecture, spec-before-code from TDD requirement) and not just transcribe user input verbatim.
baseline_gaps: transcribes user input verbatim without deriving implied principles
puts 'no inline styles' in Core Principles instead of Architecture Guardrails
omits the performance budget from quality gates or states it only in prose
does not ask clarifying questions when critical information (test coverage threshold) is missing
expected_behavior: derives at least 3 Core Principles from the provided constraints (TDD → spec-before-code, Tailwind → approved stack, WCAG → accessibility gate)
places 'no inline styles' in section IV Architecture Guardrails (checkable in code review), not Core Principles
quality gates section includes: coverage threshold (asks if not provided), WCAG 2.2 AA, bundle < 200KB gzipped
technology stack table lists jQuery explicitly as Forbidden
asks at most 1 targeted question about the missing test coverage threshold before drafting
